judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 is better: it hits all four expectations, including flagging the stale ~3-hour-old snapshot and re-checking live limits, which Report 2 omits.

report_1.evidence: One scoring call per transcript × 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 tokens/call = **≈ 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M)** ... northpeak / nimbus-large | primary (**default**) | **640,000** | needs 9.36M | **Short by ~8.72M ... would blow the window**

report_2.evidence: 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 tokens per scoring call = **9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M)** ... **northpeak** (nimbus-large) | primary — **default** | **640,000** (of 10,000,000) | 9,360,000 | **No**

report_1.evidence:
Ridgeline has 10.8M remaining — enough for the full ~9.36M run — so the heavy work should be routed there:

```
python3 transcript-scoring/scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary
```

This routes the ~9.36M-token run to ridgeline / atlas-mini, which is the only backend with the capacity to finish the full dataset
